name: Tyler Technologies Government Software API
what_it_does: Tyler Technologies REST API for enterprise government ERP, courts, public safety, and municipal management software platform. Enables AI agents to manage government ERP data access including financial, procurement, and HR management for local government, handle court case management and e-filing integration for judicial workflow automation, access public safety CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) and RMS (Records Management) data for law enforcement integration, retrieve permitting, licensing, and inspection workflow data for community development management, manage utility billing and payment processing for municipal services, handle property tax and assessment management data retrieval, access health and human services case management for social services automation, retrieve public works and asset management data for infrastructure operations, manage civic engagement and 311 service request workflows, and integrate Tyler data with state and federal reporting systems.
use_cases: Accessing government financial data from ERP integration agents
Managing court e-filing from judicial workflow agents
Retrieving public safety records from law enforcement integration agents
Processing permits and inspections from community development agents
not_for: Private sector enterprise software without government and public sector focus
Federal government without local and state government context
Consumer civic tools without enterprise government software integration
best_when: A local government agency using Tyler Technologies software wants AI agents to automate ERP integration, court case management, public safety data access, permitting workflows, and state/federal reporting.
avoid_when: GOVERNMENT COMPLIANCE RISK: Government software automation must comply with CJIS (criminal justice data), HIPAA (health data), and state-specific data governance requirements. Court and public safety data has strict access controls; unauthorized access violates federal law. Government ERP financial automation requires segregation of duties controls and audit trails.

auth.notes: Tyler Technologies uses OAuth 2.0 authentication. Product-specific API clients per Tyler software product (Munis ERP, Courts, New World EMS, etc.). Developer portal at developer.tylertech.com. Tyler Payments API for municipal payment processing. Open Data Platform for public data. Government agency customers access API through product subscriptions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — API access included with government software subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual government subscription; API access per product module
pricing.notes: Plano, Texas. Founded 1966. Public company (TYL, NYSE). Government software market leader. $1.9B+ revenue (2023). 43,000+ government clients. Products: Munis (ERP), Socrata (open data), Tyler Courts, New World (public safety), EnerGov (permitting), Appraisal & Tax. Acquired Socrata (2018) for open data. Largest government software company in North America. Competes with Oracle and SAP for government ERP.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client and per product; documented per product API

known_agent_gotchas: GOVERNMENT COMPLIANCE: CJIS data (criminal justice) requires FBI-mandated access controls; HIPAA for health services; violating data classification rules has serious legal consequences
Product-specific APIs — Tyler has 20+ software products each with separate API surfaces; Munis ERP, Courts, New World, EnerGov APIs are not interchangeable
Government change management — government software updates follow jurisdiction-specific change management processes; API version changes require agency IT approval
Financial segregation of duties — ERP financial automation must respect configured approval limits and segregation of duties; bypassing controls violates government audit requirements
Public safety data sensitivity — court and law enforcement record data has strict access controls; unauthorized API access violates CJIS and may be criminal offense
On-premises vs. SaaS — many Tyler government customers run on-premises deployments; API surface and endpoints differ from cloud; verify deployment type before integrating
agent_readiness.security_notes: Government ERP and public safety. FedRAMP, SOC2, CJIS, HIPAA. OAuth2. US. Government ERP, court, and public safety data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Government software. FedRAMP, CJIS, HIPAA. OAuth2. US. Government ERP, court, and public safety data.
